expectations.text: Total income is correctly calculated as $10,500.00 (5500 + 3200 + 1800)
expectations.evidence: Output states 'Total Income: $10,500.00' with breakdown of Payroll Deposit $5,500.00, Client Invoice Payment $3,200.00, Consulting Fee $1,800.00

expectations.text: Total expenses are correctly calculated (sum of all negative amounts)
expectations.evidence: Output states 'Total Expenses: $3,338.05' which matches the sum of all negative amounts

expectations.text: Spending is broken down by category with at least 5 categories shown
expectations.evidence: 9 expense categories shown in a table: Housing, Insurance, Utilities, Groceries, Shopping, Transportation, Food & Drink, Health, Entertainment

expectations.text: Net cash flow / surplus is calculated (income minus expenses)
expectations.evidence: Output states 'Net Cash Flow: +$7,161.95' which equals $10,500.00 - $3,338.05

expectations.text: Uses pandas for the analysis rather than manual calculation
expectations.evidence: Code block shows 'import pandas as pd', 'pd.read_csv', 'df.groupby', 'df['date'].dt.to_period' - all pandas operations following the skill pattern

claims.evidence: Table shows exactly 9 expense categories matching the CSV data
CSV data has no null values across all 20 rows and 5 columns

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-constructed for this eval. The pandas requirement differentiates with_skill from without_skill effectively.
